KidCare - What It Is & How It Works for Parents What is KidCare? KidCare is a child healthcare management app designed specifically for parents and guardians. Think of it as your child's complete health companion that lives in your pocket it helps you track your child's growth, book doctor appointments, manage vaccinations, and stay on top of everything health-related, all from one simple app. Instead of juggling paper records, calling clinics, and trying to remember when the last vaccine was given KidCare puts it all in one place, organised, accessible, and easy to use.

remember when the last vaccine was given KidCare puts it all in one place, organised, accessible, and easy to use. The Parent/Guardian User Flow - How You Actually Use It Here's what the experience looks like when a parent opens the app for the first time and starts using it: Step 1 - Set Up Your Child's Profile The first thing you do is create a profile for your child. You add their name, gender, and date of birth. This becomes their personal health card inside the app.

your child. You add their name, gender, and date of birth. This becomes their personal health card inside the app. In the staging environment, we can see a profile set up for Jhon Tesla, a newborn (DOB: 24 May 2026) with fields ready for weight and height to be filled in. Step 2 - Explore the Home Dashboard Once the profile is set up, you land on the main home screen. It's clean and easy to navigate. At a glance you can see your child's profile card, upcoming appointments, and quick-access buttons for Growth Chart, Add Details, and Vaccination.

glance you can see your child's profile card, upcoming appointments, and quick-access buttons for Growth Chart, Add Details, and Vaccination. The top navigation bar gives you instant access to the 5 key sections of the app. Step 3 - Book a Doctor Appointment When your child is unwell, you tap the appointment/call icon in the navigation. You're taken to the Appointments screen where you can see Upcoming, Completed, and Cancelled appointments all in one place.

navigation. You're taken to the Appointments screen where you can see Upcoming, Completed, and Cancelled appointments all in one place. To book a new one, you tap New Appointment, select your child's profile, and then go through a Symptoms selection screen where you describe what your child is experiencing before continuing. This helps the care partner or doctor prepare before the consultation. Step 4 - Track Growth & Add Health Details Back on the home dashboard, the child's profile card shows Age, Weight, and Height.

Track Growth & Add Health Details Back on the home dashboard, the child's profile card shows Age, Weight, and Height. Parents can log this data over time, and the Growth Chart button visualises how the child is developing making it easy to spot if anything needs attention. Step 5 - Manage Vaccinations The Vaccination button on the child's profile card takes parents to a vaccination tracker so you always know which vaccines have been given and which ones are coming up.

parents to a vaccination tracker so you always know which vaccines have been given and which ones are coming up. Step 6 - Manage Your Account & Preferences The Profile/Settings section (the last tab in the nav bar) lets parents manage everything about their account. This includes their Parent Profile, Privacy Settings, Notifications, and a Do Not Disturb toggle called Call Preference which when switched on, blocks calls from CarePartners. There's also Help & Support, FAQs, and a Privacy Policy section.

Preference which when switched on, blocks calls from CarePartners. There's also Help & Support, FAQs, and a Privacy Policy section. Core Features - Broken Down Simply Child Profile Management Every child gets their own digital health profile with name, gender, date of birth, weight, and height. Parents can manage multiple children from one account making it ideal for families with more than one kid. Appointment Booking Parents can book doctor or CarePartner appointments directly through the app. The booking flow includes a symptom selector so the consultation is already informed before it even begins.

through the app. The booking flow includes a symptom selector so the consultation is already informed before it even begins. All appointments are tracked under three categories Upcoming, Completed, and Cancelled so nothing gets lost or forgotten. Growth Chart A visual tracker that plots a child's height and weight over time. This is especially valuable in the early years when growth milestones matter most. Parents can see at a glance whether their child's development is on track. Vaccination Tracker Keeps a complete record of all vaccinations what's been done and what's due next.

development is on track. Vaccination Tracker Keeps a complete record of all vaccinations what's been done and what's due next. No more digging through paper booklets or trying to remember dates. Flip Journey & Flip Messages This is one of the more unique features visible on the home screen. Parents can Create a Flip or continue a Flip Journey which appears to be a guided care or wellness journey for the child, with messaging between the parent and a CarePartner (like a health coach or paediatrician).

or wellness journey for the child, with messaging between the parent and a CarePartner (like a health coach or paediatrician). It adds a personal, ongoing relationship dimension to the app rather than just transactional appointments. CarePartner Calls The app connects parents directly with CarePartners likely trained health professionals or paediatric support staff. Parents can control when they're reachable via the Call Preference / DND toggle, so they're never disturbed at inconvenient times.

Parents can control when they're reachable via the Call Preference / DND toggle, so they're never disturbed at inconvenient times. Notifications & Privacy Controls Parents have full control over their notification preferences and privacy settings — giving them peace of mind that their child's health data is handled carefully and on their own terms. Value Proposition-Why KidCare Matters KidCare solves a very real problem that every parent faces: child healthcare is scattered, stressful, and hard to keep on top of. Here's what makes it genuinely valuable: Everything in one place.

Growth records, appointments, vaccinations, and health notes all stored digitally and always accessible, whether you're at home or at the clinic. Proactive, not just reactive. The Flip Journey feature suggests that KidCare isn't just for when your child is sick. It's designed to support ongoing child development and wellness making it a long-term companion, not just an emergency tool. A real human connection. The CarePartner model means parents aren't just using a cold, automated system.

FloCard - Smart, Sustainable Digital Business Cards What problem it solves: Billions of paper business cards are printed, exchanged, and thrown in the bin every single year it's wasteful, expensive to reprint every time your details change, and frankly outdated in a world where everything else is digital. On top of that, businesses increasingly need to show real commitment to sustainability, not just talk about it.

is digital. On top of that, businesses increasingly need to show real commitment to sustainability, not just talk about it. FloCard solves both problems at once by replacing the paper card with a smart, eco-friendly digital identity you can share instantly from your phone. Who uses it: Professionals, freelancers, entrepreneurs, and businesses of all sizes from a solo developer wanting a sleek way to share their contact info at a networking event, to a whole company rolling out digital cards for their entire team. It works on Web3 browser, Android, and iOS, so it meets people wherever they already are.

for their entire team. It works on Web3 browser, Android, and iOS, so it meets people wherever they already are. What's the core value delivered: FloCard turns the simple act of sharing your contact details into something smarter and more meaningful. You create your card once name, role, photo, social links, website and from that moment on, you share it via QR code, email, or a unique numeric code. Your details are always current, you never run out of cards, and every exchange signals to the other person that you're forward-thinking and environmentally conscious.

you never run out of cards, and every exchange signals to the other person that you're forward-thinking and environmentally conscious. The built-in Flo Balance and transaction system makes it feel like a living professional network, not a static card. And because the Planters App is built right inside FloCard, every card you create is connected to something bigger real trees being planted in the real world. 2.

Tree Planters (Planters App) - Making Corporate Sustainability Real and Traceable What problem it solves: Most companies want to be more sustainable they have Net Zero targets, CSR commitments, and ESG goals on paper but turning those intentions into something real, verifiable, and trackable on the ground is genuinely hard. Tree planting projects often lack transparency: businesses fund them but have no way to know if the trees were actually planted, where they are, or whether they survived.

them but have no way to know if the trees were actually planted, where they are, or whether they survived. The Planters App fixes exactly this by bringing cutting-edge technology and full transparency to every single restoration project because as they put it, "every tree counts, so we count every tree." Who uses it: Two distinct groups of people. First, businesses companies with sustainability goals, Net Zero commitments, or corporate social responsibility targets who want to fund real, traceable reforestation projects with precision and integrity.

Net Zero commitments, or corporate social responsibility targets who want to fund real, traceable reforestation projects with precision and integrity. Second, on-the-ground planters the actual people doing the planting work who use the app to manage plantation projects, log their work, and track progress in the field. It bridges both sides of the same mission, connecting the business that funds the trees with the planter who puts them in the ground. What's the core value delivered: The Planters App gives sustainability something it's always been missing proof.

them in the ground. What's the core value delivered: The Planters App gives sustainability something it's always been missing proof. It's not enough to say you've planted trees; the app makes every restoration effort impactful, traceable, and sustainable from start to finish. For businesses, it means their environmental commitments aren't just a line in an annual report they're real, verified, and trackable. For planters, it means their work is professionally managed and their contribution is recorded.

report they're real, verified, and trackable. For planters, it means their work is professionally managed and their contribution is recorded. And because it lives inside the FloCard ecosystem, every FloCard user already has a door into this world meaning the simple switch from paper to digital cards is just the beginning of a much larger commitment to the planet. 3.

KidCare - Your Child's Health, Always in Your Hands What problem it solves: Parenting is already one of the most demanding jobs in the world and keeping on top of a child's vaccinations, growth milestones, and doctor appointments on top of everything else adds a whole extra layer of stress. Most parents are still juggling paper vaccination booklets, clinic phone calls, and mental notes about when the last check-up was.

Most parents are still juggling paper vaccination booklets, clinic phone calls, and mental notes about when the last check-up was. Things get forgotten, records get lost, and by the time you realise a vaccine was due last month, the anxiety has already set in. KidCare puts all of that into one simple, organised app so nothing ever gets missed and nothing feels overwhelming. Who uses it: Parents and guardians of young children especially new parents navigating those critical first months and years when growth tracking, vaccination schedules, and regular health check-ups matter most.

new parents navigating those critical first months and years when growth tracking, vaccination schedules, and regular health check-ups matter most. It's designed to be simple enough for anyone to use, because when your child is unwell at midnight you don't want to figure out a complicated interface you want answers and help, fast. What's the core value delivered: KidCare gives parents something priceless genuine peace of mind. From the moment you set up your child's profile, everything lives in one place: their growth chart, vaccination history, upcoming appointments, and a direct line to a CarePartner who knows your child's background.

place: their growth chart, vaccination history, upcoming appointments, and a direct line to a CarePartner who knows your child's background. You can book a consultation in under a minute with symptoms already logged before the doctor even picks up. The Flip Journey feature takes it a step further it turns the relationship with your CarePartner into an ongoing, guided wellness journey rather than a series of disconnected one-off appointments. And the DND / Call Preference toggle means you stay in control of when you're reachable, so the app works around your life, not the other way around.

ACT 5 Scene 1 Contents Michael Witmore Director, Folger Shakespeare Library It is hard to imagine a world without Shakespeare. Since their composition four hundred years ago, Shakespeare’s plays and poems have traveled the globe, inviting those who see and read his works to make them their own. Readers of the New Folger Editions are part of this ongoing process of “taking up Shakespeare,” finding our own thoughts and feelings in language that strikes us as old or unusual and, for that very reason, new.

our own thoughts and feelings in language that strikes us as old or unusual and, for that very reason, new. We still struggle to keep up with a writer who could think a mile a minute, whose words paint pictures that shift like clouds. These expertly edited texts are presented to the public as a resource for study, artistic adaptation, and enjoyment. By making the classic texts of the New Folger Editions available in electronic form as Folger Digital Texts, we place a trusted resource in the hands of anyone who wants them.

in electronic form as Folger Digital Texts, we place a trusted resource in the hands of anyone who wants them. The New Folger Editions of Shakespeare’s plays, which are the basis for the texts realized here in digital form, are special because of their origin. The Folger Shakespeare Library in Washington, DC, is the single greatest documentary source of Shakespeare’s works. An unparalleled collection of early modern books, manuscripts, and artwork connected to Shakespeare, the Folger’s holdings have been consulted extensively in the preparation of these texts.

books, manuscripts, and artwork connected to Shakespeare, the Folger’s holdings have been consulted extensively in the preparation of these texts. The Editions also reflect the expertise gained through the regular performance of Shakespeare’s works in the Folger’s Elizabethan Theater. I want to express my deep thanks to editors Barbara Mowat and Paul Werstine for creating these indispensable editions of Shakespeare’s works, which incorporate the best of textual scholarship with a richness of commentary that is both inspired and engaging.

Shakespeare’s works, which incorporate the best of textual scholarship with a richness of commentary that is both inspired and engaging. Readers who want to know more about Shakespeare and his plays can follow the paths these distinguished scholars have tread by visiting the Folger either in-person or online, where a range of physical and digital resources exist to supplement the material in these texts. I commend to you these words, and hope that they inspire.

From the Director of the Folger Shakespeare Library Until now, with the release of the Folger Digital Texts, readers in search of a free online text of Shakespeare’s plays had to be content primarily with using the Moby™ Text, which reproduces a late- nineteenth century version of the plays. What is the difference? Many ordinary readers assume that there is a single text for the plays: what Shakespeare wrote.

What is the difference? Many ordinary readers assume that there is a single text for the plays: what Shakespeare wrote. But Shakespeare’s plays were not published the way modern novels or plays are published today: as a single, authoritative text. In some cases, the plays have come down to us in multiple published versions, represented by various Quartos (Qq) and by the great collection put together by his colleagues in 1623, called the First Folio (F). There are, for example, three very different versions of Hamlet, two of King Lear, Henry V, Romeo and Juliet, and others.

are, for example, three very different versions of Hamlet, two of King Lear, Henry V, Romeo and Juliet, and others. Editors choose which version to use as their base text, and then amend that text with words, lines or speech prefixes from the other versions that, in their judgment, make for a better or more accurate text.

lines or speech prefixes from the other versions that, in their judgment, make for a better or more accurate text. Other editorial decisions involve choices about whether an unfamiliar word could be understood in light of other writings of the period or whether it should be changed; decisions about words that made it into Shakespeare’s text by accident through four hundred years of printings and misprinting; and even decisions based on cultural preference and taste. When the Moby™ Text was created, for example, it was deemed “improper” and “indecent” for Miranda to chastise Caliban for having attempted to rape her.

created, for example, it was deemed “improper” and “indecent” for Miranda to chastise Caliban for having attempted to rape her. (See The Tempest, 1.2: “Abhorred slave,/Which any print of goodness wilt not take,/Being capable of all ill! I pitied thee…”). All Shakespeare editors at the time took the speech away from her and gave it to her father, Prospero. The editors of the Moby™ Shakespeare produced their text long before scholars fully understood the proper grounds on which to make the thousands of decisions that Shakespeare editors face.

long before scholars fully understood the proper grounds on which to make the thousands of decisions that Shakespeare editors face. The Folger Library Shakespeare Editions, on which the Folger Digital Texts depend, make this editorial process as nearly transparent as is possible, in contrast to older texts, like the Moby™, which hide editorial interventions.

editorial process as nearly transparent as is possible, in contrast to older texts, like the Moby™, which hide editorial interventions. The reader of the Folger Shakespeare knows where the text has been altered because editorial interventions are signaled by square brackets (for example, from Othello: “ If she in chains of magic were not bound, ”), half-square brackets (for example, from Henry V: “With blood and sword and fire to win your Textual Introduction By Barbara Mowat and Paul Werstine p , y y right,”), or angle brackets (for example, from Hamlet: “O farewell, honest soldier. Who hath relieved/you?”).

Werstine p , y y right,”), or angle brackets (for example, from Hamlet: “O farewell, honest soldier. Who hath relieved/you?”). At any point in the text, you can hover your cursor over a bracket for more information. Antonio, the merchant in The Merchant of Venice, secures a loan from Shylock for his friend Bassanio, who seeks to court Portia. Shylock, a Jewish moneylender, recalls past insults from Antonio and, instead of asking interest on the loan, asks instead—in what he calls a “merry sport”—that if the loan is not repaid, Antonio will owe a pound of his own flesh. Bassanio sails to Belmont, where the wealthy heiress Portia is being courted by suitors from around the world.

own flesh. Bassanio sails to Belmont, where the wealthy heiress Portia is being courted by suitors from around the world. Her father’s will requires that the successful suitor solve a riddle involving chests of gold, silver, and lead. Where others have failed, Bassanio succeeds by selecting the right chest. Portia marries Bassanio; her waiting woman, Nerissa, marries his friend Gratiano. Shylock’s daughter, Jessica, has eloped with Bassanio’s friend Lorenzo, taking her father’s money with her. Shylock is devastated. When Antonio cannot repay the loan, Shylock demands the pound of flesh. When the news reaches Belmont, Bassanio returns to Venice.

Chapter 3: Theoretical Background Before building any system, especially something based on AI, it’s important to understand the basic ideas behind it. Not in a very complicated way, but enough to know what’s actually happening inside. Because sometimes these terms sound very technical, but the core idea is actually quite simple. Artificial Intelligence, or AI, is basically about making machines capable of doing tasks that normally require human intelligence. It doesn’t mean machines are “thinking” like humans exactly, but they are trying to imitate certain aspects of it. Not perfectly though. One of the main parts of AI is Machine Learning. This is where systems learn from data instead of being directly programmed for every task. For example, instead of telling a system exactly how to predict house prices, we give it past data. Supervised learning is one of the most commonly used. In this, the model is trained using labeled data. For example, past property records where price is already known. The system learns the relationship between input (like location, size) and output (price). Then it tries to predict for new data. Sometimes it works really well, sometimes not so much. Another type is unsupervised learning.

In a real office, you have filing cabinets, notice boards, meeting rooms, and department folders. SharePoint is all of that but online, organized, searchable, and accessible from anywhere. It's a platform built by Microsoft that lets teams store files, share information, automate work, and collaborate all in one place, connected to tools like Microsoft Teams, Outlook, and Office apps. Document Libraries - Your Smart Filing Cabinet A Document Library is basically a folder system - but a really smart one. In a normal folder (like on your desktop or Google Drive), you just dump files in. SharePoint libraries go much further: Every file can have metadata tags - like Department, Status, Project Name, Author -so you can filter and search without opening anything. Version history is automatic. If someone edits a Word doc, the previous 10 versions are saved.

Understanding Git Basics While learning Git, I understood that it is much more than just a tool for saving code. Git helps developers track changes, collaborate with teams, and maintain different versions of a project safely. Here are the main concepts I explored: 🔹 Staging Staging allows developers to select specific files or changes before saving them into project history. It acts like a preparation area before committing changes. Example: git add . 🔹 Commits A commit is like taking a snapshot of the project at a specific point in time.

add . 🔹 Commits A commit is like taking a snapshot of the project at a specific point in time. Every commit stores changes along with a message describing what was updated. Example: git commit -m "Added README file" 🔹 Push Push uploads local project changes from the developer’s system to the remote GitHub repository so the team can access updated code. Example: git push origin main 🔹 Pull Pull downloads the latest changes from the remote repository to the local system so developers stay updated with team progress.

Pull downloads the latest changes from the remote repository to the local system so developers stay updated with team progress. Example: git pull origin main 🔹 Merge Merge combines changes from different branches into a single branch. This is important when feature development is completed and needs to be integrated into the main project. Example: git merge feature/login-page Branching Strategies I also learned about how teams organize development using branches. 🔹 main Branch The main branch usually contains the stable and production-ready version of the project.

organize development using branches. 🔹 main Branch The main branch usually contains the stable and production-ready version of the project. 🔹 dev Branch The dev branch is used for ongoing development where new updates are tested before going to production. 🔹 feature/* Branches Feature branches are created for individual tasks or features like: feature/login feature/payment-ui feature/profile-update This keeps development organized and prevents unfinished work from affecting the stable version.

tasks or features like: feature/login feature/payment-ui feature/profile-update This keeps development organized and prevents unfinished work from affecting the stable version. Linl-https://youtu.be/1G2cXvgYUpE?si=8lEKcQbaaTBuP_4Z Think of SharePoint as your company's digital office building. In a real office, you have filing cabinets, notice boards, meeting rooms, and department folders. SharePoint is all of that but online, organized, searchable, and accessible from anywhere. It's a platform built by Microsoft that lets teams store files, share information, automate work, and collaborate all in one place, connected to tools like Microsoft Teams, Outlook, and Office apps. one place, connected to tools like Microsoft Teams, Outlook, and Office apps. The 3 Key Building Blocks of SharePoint 1. Document Libraries - Your Smart Filing Cabinet A Document Library is basically a folder system - but a really smart one. In a normal folder (like on your desktop or Google Drive), you just dump files in. SharePoint libraries go much further: Every file can have metadata tags - like Department, Status, Project Name, Author -so you can filter and search without opening anything. Version history is automatic. If someone edits a Word doc, the previous 10 versions are saved.

search without opening anything. Version history is automatic. If someone edits a Word doc, the previous 10 versions are saved. You can roll back any time. Files are locked when someone is editing - no more "who has the file open?" situations. You can set permissions - some people can only read, others can edit, others can upload but not delete. Files sync to your desktop via OneDrive, so it feels like a normal folder locally. Real example: Your HR team stores all employee policies in a SharePoint library.

it feels like a normal folder locally. Real example: Your HR team stores all employee policies in a SharePoint library. Everyone in the company can read them, but only HR can edit them. When a policy is updated, everyone automatically gets the latest version - no emailing PDFs back and forth. 2. Sites - Your Team's Own Mini-Website A SharePoint Site is like giving each team or project their own dedicated webpage inside your company.

Team's Own Mini-Website A SharePoint Site is like giving each team or project their own dedicated webpage inside your company. It can include: Document libraries (files) Announcements and news Calendars and events Links to useful tools Task lists Embedded dashboards or reports There are two types: Team Sites - private, for a specific team (e.g., Finance Team, HR, IT). Linked to a Microsoft Teams group. Communication Sites - broader, like a company intranet. Used to share news, updates, and policies across the whole organization.
